methods.method_name: Sample Pre-treatment and Particle Size Distribution
methods.description: This method involves the pre-treatment of soil samples to prepare them for particle size distribution analysis using laser granulometry.

steps.description: Pre-treatment of samples involved air drying at room temperature, removal of large roots and gravel and passing through 2 mm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare soil samples by air drying, removing roots and gravel, and sieving through 2 mm

steps.description: Particle size distributions of the samples (after the removal of carbonates with dilute HCl) were performed on laser granulometry using Malvern Mastersizer 3000 connected to a Hydro MV, wet dispersion unit.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform particle size distribution analysis using laser granulometry

methods.method_name: Soil Reaction and Electrical Conductivity Measurement
methods.description: This method measures the soil reaction (pH) and electrical conductivity (EC) using a specific soil to solution ratio.

steps.description: Soil reaction (pH) and electrical conductivity (EC) were measured in a 1:2 soil to solution ratio and the values read off using pH meter and EC glass electrodes respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure pH and EC of soil using a 1:2 soil to solution ratio

methods.description: This method determines the carbonate content of soil samples gasometrically by measuring the volume of carbon dioxide evolved during reaction with hydrochloric acid.

steps.description: The carbonate content was determined gasometrically by measuring the volume of carbon dioxide evolved during reaction with hydrochloric acid using an Eijkelkamp calcimeter.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine carbonate content by measuring CO2 volume evolved with HCl

methods.description: This method analyzes the total elemental composition of soil samples using Borate Fusion and sodium peroxide fusion methods.

steps.description: The suite of major rock-forming oxides was determined by the Borate Fusion Method. A sample weighing 0.7 g was mixed with lithium tetraborate flux and fused to form a homogenous glass bead.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine major oxides using Borate Fusion Method

steps.description: The borate fusion was followed by analysis of the glass bead on the WDXRF Spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze glass bead using WDXRF Spectrometer

steps.description: The minor and trace elements were determined by sodium peroxide fusion method. Sample (0.2-2.0 g) was weighed into a crucible containing NaO2 and NaOH. The mixture was then fused.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine minor and trace elements using sodium peroxide fusion

steps.description: The sample was leached, acidified and made up to volume. The sample was then analysed by ICP-OES.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze leached and acidified sample using ICP-OES

methods.description: This method assesses elemental redistribution through weathering and pedogenesis by normalizing chemical elements by an immobile element.

steps.description: Normalization of all chemical elements of interest by an immobile element (in this study, Ti) as proposed by Brimhall and Dietrich (1987) was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize chemical elements by immobile element Ti

steps.description: Changes in provenance are detectable from Zr/Ti ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect changes in provenance using Zr/Ti ratios
